summary_text: **Findings (Severity Order)** 1. **Critical: CI is explicitly skipping core scheduler reliability tests.** In live `x-auto` CI, the `pytest` command excludes scheduler suites that cover missed-post logic, gate checks, and trend scanning: [ci.yml](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/.github/workflows/ci.yml#L68). Result: regressions in the most failure-prone path can merge undetected. 2. **High: Deadline reminder logic is incorrect and can generate false “no approved post” alerts.** Slot match uses `scheduled_for.startswith(slot_str)`, but `scheduled_for` format is `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`; this almost always fails for valid posts: [scheduler_alerts.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler_alerts.py#L119). There is no test covering this function. 3. **High: Alert storm behavior exists; duplicate missed alerts are not deduped/rate-limited by key.** `_send_alert` appends and sends every event without fingerprint TTL: [scheduler_alerts.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler_alerts.py#L20). Evidence from live log aggregation: same missed message repeated ~695 times in `alerts.jsonl` (`Missed post: (scheduled 2026-03-26 11:50)` and peers). 4. **High: Scheduler collision detection is mostly post-hoc unless health script is manually run.** PID lock exits on duplicate scheduler but does not emit immediate alert channel signal: [_acquire_pid_lock](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler_health.py#L167). Health script can fail on recent collisions, but only when invoked: [x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py#L281). 5. **Medium-High: Missed-status reconciliation can be dropped under Notion stale snapshot checks, enabling repeated re-miss cycles.** Local status dirty flags are pushed with optimistic stale guard; on mismatch updates are skipped: [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L629). Scheduler marks missed after 3 cycles: [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L518). Combined with Notion-first reload, unsynced local `missed` can be resurrected as `approved`. 6. **Medium: Operational watchdog/aux agents are defined but not loaded in launchd.** `watchdog/autogen/trend` plists exist in repo, but `launchctl print` shows services not found for all three. This weakens early detection (heartbeat stale), and removes autonomous replenishment feedback loops. 7. **Medium: Health check emits persistent non-actionable warnings in current runtime model.** It warns about missing launcher script path even though launchd uses direct python invocation: [x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py#L248). Signal/noise worsens operator trust in health output. --- **Recommended Structural Fixes** 1. **Split CI into “merge-gate” and “nightly-full” with scheduler tests mandatory in at least one lane.** Keep fast PR lane, but run `test_scheduler_r11_r12.py`, `test_scheduler_gate1.py`, `test_trend_scanner.py` in nightly and before release tags. Promote any failure to blocking for deployment branches. 2. **Introduce event-level dedupe for alerts.** Add `event_key + TTL` suppression (e.g., `kind=missed`, `post_id`, `scheduled_for`) before Telegram/Slack emit. Store state in local `alerts_state.json`. 3. **Make collision a first-class alert event.** On PID-lock collision, emit `_send_alert` once per TTL window with competing PID and parent process details. 4. **Harden status reconciliation semantics.** For terminal operational statuses (`missed`, `failed`, `duplicate_blocked`), add a guaranteed Notion write retry queue (idempotent patch jobs) so local terminalization cannot be silently dropped by stale-snapshot skips. 5. **Operationalize watchdog path.** Load and monitor `com.cursorvers.x-auto-watchdog`; feed stale-heartbeat events into same alert dedupe system. 6. **Add explicit SLO telemetry.** Emit structured counters to JSONL: `slot_due`, `slot_posted`, `slot_missed`, `validation_fail`, `collision_detected`, `notion_push_skipped_stal...
